---
id: "19.2.5"
title: "Disk Failure Tracking on HCI Clusters"
criticality: "critical"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.2.5 · Disk Failure Tracking on HCI Clusters

## Description

Unifies per-physical-disk telemetry from vSphere, Nutanix, and HyperFlex, adds governance from `disk_owner` (loaded through `| join ... [ | inputlookup disk_owner ]` in the canonical SPL) with `expected_disk_count` for silent disk loss, and classifies a five-tier `severity_bucket` with rolling 1h, 24h, and 7d failure rates plus predictive disk counts, optional wear percentage, and change-freeze demotion that never suppresses a true critical. This is not UC-19.2.17 (vSAN disk group mount and checksums), not UC-19.2.7 (CVM Stargate health), not UC-19.2.27 (vSAN free space and headroom), and not UC-19.2.1 (HyperFlex whole-cluster story without per-disk focus). The canonical SPL uses `multisearch` so a short `earliest=-2h@h` branch captures fresh inventory while a seven-day branch feeds failure and prediction math.

## Value

A single bad disk in an HCI array usually fails inside the resiliency policy, but a second fault on the same host, a long rebuild after slow SSD wear, or a silent count gap in the management API is how weekend batches and clinical imaging queues cross from delayed I/O to full downtime. Tying disk-level signals to expected inventory from CMDB gives finance, storage, and application owners a shared row that is numerically comparable to the vSphere, Prism, and HX UIs, so replacement parts and rebuild windows are scheduled before a maintenance freeze collides with double faults.

## Implementation

Install the Nutanix TA to collectors and Search Heads, add VMware forwarders for vSphere events, and deploy a heavy-forwarder app that posts `vmware:vsan:diskgroup` lines from the `/disks` and `/health` REST surfaces plus the HyperFlex `cisco:hyperflex:disk` poller. Place `lookups/disk_owner.csv` with a `transforms.conf` `disk_owner` entry. Paste the `spl` into a report, set the time picker to Last 7 days, verify inventory branch uses `earliest=-2h@h` and failure branch `earliest=-7d@d`, then schedule a five- or fifteen-minute alert with throttling on `cluster_name` and `severity_bucket`, routing critical and any non-zero `disk_loss` to PagerDuty, high to ServiceNow P2, and lower tiers to Slack and digest mail.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites

In HCI, a “disk failure” is almost never an instant full-cluster outage. Replication, erasure, or mirroring (RF2, RF3, or vSAN policies) means one physical loss shows up as rebuild traffic, a dip in resiliency, and time pressure—not necessarily power-off for every guest. What does become an outage is the stack of: second disk in the same fault domain, slow rebuilds because uplinks or cache saturation, SSD wear that suddenly exhausts a whole batch, and administrative blind spots when the control-plane API still answers “green” but one fewer disk object returns than the CMDB says should exist. You must know three vSAN per-disk `health.state` values from the `/disks` API: `Healthy` is serving I/O, `UnhealthyOnline` is impaired but still visible to vSAN, and `UnhealthyOffline` is a hard fault path. Nutanix uses `kNormal`, `kPredictiveFailure` for early SMART-like trips, `kMarkedForRemoval` when a disk is in or awaiting a planned pull, and `kOffline` when the disk is not participating. HyperFlex per-disk `healthState` enumerates 1=healthy, 2=warning, 3=critical. Hot-swap evacuation is change-managed and often paired with a maintenance window; predictive failure is the vendor and SMART telling you to act before a timeout; a hard failure is a sudden `diskFailed` or `kOffline` with rebuild storms behind it.

A per-cluster `expected_disk_count` in CMDB is not optional for mature operations: the REST inventory call can legally return a shorter list after a field swap or a dead disk bay even when no alert fired yet, and the gap between that count and your fleet inventory is a silent loss metric your CFO-level dashboards will miss if you only watch vendor health. The `disk_owner` lookup (CSV or KV) must be queryable as `| inputlookup disk_owner` and must at minimum include: `cluster_name` (same string the pollers and Prism use), `vendor` (vmware, nutanix, cisco, or mixed site tag), `expected_disk_count` (integer, total physical data disks the cluster should show when fully populated), `change_freeze_until` (empty when none, else ISO-8601 or date-only for CAB or finance close), `business_service` (app id or runbook), `owner_email` (service owner), and `redundancy_failures_allowed` (default 1 for a simple “more than this simultaneous loss is critical” rule in Step 2). `default_rf` is optional, used as a string-form replication factor (2 or 3) to remind operators how many copies exist when you set `rfa` in the SPL. Keep an optional `site_code` column for later joins even if the base alert does not use it. Case-normalise `cluster_name` in the poller if your CMDB and vCenter disagree on case.

This UC is intentionally narrow: physical drive failure, prediction, and silent count loss, not the disk group or capacity-only stories listed in the exclusions. Optional `nix:smart` from the Linux TA is a wear-projection add-on, not a primary NetApp-class warranty signal.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

1A. vSAN physical-disk poller (Python 3, standard library only). Run on a Heavy Forwarder or a hardened Linux management host, store vCenter and HEC secrets outside the repo, and install your enterprise root into `ctx.load_verify_locations`. The example posts one JSON per poll with `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:diskgroup`, `object_kind=physical_disk`, and `api_path=disks` to avoid collision with a disk group feed that still reuses the same name on another index.

```python
import base64, json, ssl, sys, time, urllib.error, urllib.request

def hec_post(ctx, hec, token, payload_obj, st, source, index_name):
    tsec = int(time.time())
    event = json.dumps(payload_obj, separators=(",", ":"))
    body = json.dumps({"time": tsec, "sourcetype": st, "source": source, "index": index_name, "event": event}).encode("utf-8")
    req = urllib.request.Request(hec, data=body, method="POST")
    req.add_header("Authorization", "Splunk " + token)
    req.add_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=60) as r:
        r.read()

def vsphere_session(ctx, vcenter, user, password):
    pair = (user + ":" + password).encode("utf-8")
    token = base64.b64encode(pair).decode("ascii")
    url = vcenter.rstrip("/") + "/api/session"
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=b"{}", method="POST")
    req.add_header("Authorization", "Basic " + token)
    req.add_header("Content-Type", "application/json")
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=60) as r:
        sid = r.read().decode("utf-8").strip().strip('"')
    return sid

def vsphere_get(ctx, vcenter, sid, path):
    url = vcenter.rstrip("/") + path
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, method="GET")
    req.add_header("vmware-api-session-id", sid)
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=60) as r:
        return r.read().decode("utf-8")

def main():
    vcenter = "https://VCENTER_FQDN"
    hec = "https://HEC:8088/services/collector/event"
    hec_tok = "HEC-TOKEN-REPLACE"
    user = "svc.splunk.read"
    password = "REPLACE"
    ca = "/etc/pki/splunk/vcenter-chain.pem"
    index_name = "vmware"
    st = "vmware:vsan:diskgroup"
    source = "vsan:disks_poll"
    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
    ctx.load_verify_locations(cafile=ca)
    try:
        sid = vsphere_session(ctx, vcenter, user, password)
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        if e.code == 401:
            sys.exit(3)
        raise
    clusters_path = "/api/vcenter/cluster?filter=true"
    try:
        cj = json.loads(vsphere_get(ctx, vcenter, sid, clusters_path))
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        if e.code in (401, 429, 503):
            sys.exit(4 if e.code == 429 else 5)
        raise
    for cl in cj:
        cid = cl.get("cluster") or cl.get("cluster_id")
        if not cid:
            continue
        p = "/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/" + cid + "/disks"
        try:
            raw = vsphere_get(ctx, vcenter, sid, p)
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code in (401, 429, 503):
                continue
            raise
        if not raw:
            continue
        for row in json.loads(raw):
            row["clusterId"] = cid
            row["api_path"] = "disks"
            row["object_kind"] = "physical_disk"
            try:
                hec_post(ctx, hec, hec_tok, row, st, source, index_name)
            except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
                if e.code in (401, 429, 503):
                    time.sleep(5)
            time.sleep(0.05)
    return 0

if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        main()
    except Exception:
        raise
```

1B. vSAN health test poller: call GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/health, filter JSON for `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.physicaldiskhealth` and `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.diskbalance`, and post each with `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:health` including `test_id` and the cluster name string your lookup expects.

1C. VMware vSphere events: on the same Heavy Forwarder that runs 5380, confirm `vmware:vc:event` includes `EventType` or the snake_case variant for `LostStorageEvent`, `DegradedHostStorageEvent`, `vsan.host.diskAdded`, and `vsan.host.diskFailed`. If a field rename occurred on upgrade, add a `FIELDALIAS` in your wrapper app, not in vendor `default`.

1D. Nutanix: install `Splunk_TA_nutanix` from 5076, open Prism/PC to the documented ports, and enable the modular input families that emit `nutanix:cluster:disks` and `nutanix:cluster:alerts` into `index=nutanix` (or your chosen hci index) with JSON extraction. Example `local/inputs.conf` fragment—adjust stanza names to your TA build:

```ini
[http://NUTANIX-PRISM-JSON]
index = nutanix
disabled = 0
```

1E. props for Nutanix JSON in your wrapper app, not the vendor default you never edit in place:

```ini
[nutanix:cluster:disks]
TRUNCATE = 2000000
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
```

1F. HyperFlex disk: extend `TA_hx_collector` to call `GET /rest/clusters/{cluster_id}/disks` with the same `Authorization: Bearer` flow as other HX paths, and write `cisco:hyperflex:disk` and `cisco:hyperflex:alarm` to `index=hyperflex`. Use the `cluster_id` from `GET /rest/clusters` to parameterise the disk call so you are not hand-maintaining a static list. Keep the CA file path on disk for TLS, not a disabled verifier.

1G. Optional `nix:smart` on a Linux SSG: enable the `smart` scripted input in `Splunk_TA_nix` and route to `index=os`, knowing guests lack raw NVMe; this base UC does not require that stream for a green evidence review.

1H. transforms for disk governance:

```ini
[disk_owner]
filename = disk_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
```

`metadata/local.meta` should export the lookup to roles that run the search.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Paste the `spl` field into Search. It begins with a `comment` macro for audit, then a `multisearch` where the first three bracketed arms use `earliest=-2h@h` to pull fresh disk inventory and field-normalised `dkey` values for VMware, Nutanix, and HyperFlex, and the final arm uses `earliest=-7d@d` to gather failure, health, alert, and optional SMART events with f1, f24, and f7 flags derived from `relative_time` windows. The join key is `cluster_name` and `vendor` after a uniform `coalesce` on camel and snake case. The `inputlookup` equivalent is implemented as a `lookup` to `disk_owner` on the rolled-up stats row so you do not have to re-run the subsearch. `redundancy_failures_allowed` powers the simple N+1 style rule where `disk_loss` greater than the allowed value is a critical class alongside concurrent failures. The change-freeze line maps `change_freeze_until` into a timestamp, sets `freeze_active`, and rewrites non-critical severities one step lower, always leaving a `critical` class row at `critical` when freeze is active, matching your CAB policy.

**Search walkthrough** — The `multisearch` isolates a short inventory lookback for accurate `current_disk_count` and a long failure lookback in one pass without double `stats` on unrelated windows. The coalesce and vendor case logic absorb naming drift as Cisco and Broadcom change JSON keys. The failure arm keeps OR logic in the base search in uppercase. The risk stats command aggregates failure counters and predictive `mv` lists per cluster, then the lookup enriches with CMDB and freeze fields. A five-part `case` for `sev0` maps the user contract: several simultaneous failures, disk loss over redundancy, and wear at 100% are classed as the highest concern; a single sub-hour failure or predictive set goes high; medium holds predictive singles, 90% wear, and mild disk loss; low carries historical-only failures. After freeze remapping, the final `table` is the one you paste into dashboards, ITSI, and ServiceNow webhooks. Schedule every five to fifteen minutes, throttle on `cluster_name` for thirty to sixty minutes except when `severity_bucket` escalates from low to high in the same night.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.5 — HCI physical disk failure: multisearch with earliest=-2h@h for inventory, earliest=-7d@d for failure; join uses inputlookup disk_owner; five-tier case severity; freeze does not down-class critical.")`
| multisearch [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval ap=toString(coalesce(api_path, apiPath, object_kind, objectKind, ""))
  | where (ap="disks") OR (ap="physical_disk") OR match(ap, "(?i)physical") OR ((isnull(diskGroupUuid) OR len(toString(diskGroupUuid))=0) AND (isnotnull(naa) OR isnotnull(deviceId) OR isnotnull(device_id)))
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, clusterId, vcenter_cluster, ""))
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_key, device_key, naa, name, id, deviceId, device_id, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="vmware"
  | eval stream="inv"
  | eval wtrack=tonumber(toString(coalesce(wear_percent, wear_indicator, wearIndicator, "-1")))
] [ search (index=nutanix OR index=hci) sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, "unknown"))
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_uuid, diskUuid, disk_serial, diskSerial, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="nutanix"
  | eval stream="inv"
  | eval wtrack=-1
] [ search (index=hyperflex OR index=hci) sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:disk" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, displayName, cluster_id, "unknown"))
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_uuid, serial, diskSerial, id, name, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="cisco"
  | eval stream="inv"
  | eval wtrack=-1
] [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci OR index=nutanix OR index=hyperflex OR index=os) earliest=-7d@d
    (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:disk" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm" OR (index=os AND sourcetype="nix:smart"))
  | eval et=toString(coalesce(event_type, EventType, type, name, eventType, alert_title, title, message, test_id, ""))
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, clusterId, vcenter_cluster, "unknown"))
  | eval dstate=toString(coalesce(disk_state, diskState, ""))
  | eval hraw=lower(toString(coalesce('health.state', health_state, healthState, "")))
  | eval hxi=tonumber(toString(coalesce(healthState, health_state, "0")))
  | eval mfr=tonumber(toString(coalesce(marked_for_removal, markedForRemoval, "0")))
  | eval vendor=case(sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR match(sourcetype, "^vmware:"), "vmware", sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks", "nutanix", match(sourcetype, "cisco:hyperflex"), "cisco", sourcetype="nix:smart", "os_smart", 1=1, "other")
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_key, device_key, disk_uuid, diskUuid, naa, name, id, deviceId, device_id, "unknown"))
  | eval is_fail=case(sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" AND (et IN ("LostStorageEvent", "DegradedHostStorageEvent") OR match(et, "vsan\.host\.diskFailed")), 1, sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" AND match(lower(et), "diskfailure|diskoffline|diskmarked|disk.*fail"), 1, sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" AND dstate IN ("kOffline"), 1, (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:disk") AND ((match(hraw, "unhealthy") AND (match(hraw, "online") OR match(hraw, "offline"))) OR dstate IN ("kPredictiveFailure", "kMarkedForRemoval") OR mfr=1 OR hxi>2), 1, sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm" AND match(lower(et), "disk|media|storage|drive|ssd|nvme"), 1, 1=1, 0)
  | eval is_pred=case(dstate="kPredictiveFailure", 1, mfr=1 AND dstate!="kNormal", 1, hxi=2 OR hxi=3, 1, match(hraw, "unhealthy|predictive"), 1, 1=1, 0)
  | eval f1=if((is_fail=1) AND _time>=relative_time(now(), "-1h@h"), 1, 0)
  | eval f24=if((is_fail=1) AND _time>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h"), 1, 0)
  | eval f7=if(is_fail=1, 1, 0)
  | eval host_key=toString(coalesce(host, hostName, node_name, esx_host, "unknown"))
  | eval m_pred=if((is_pred=1) AND (len(host_key)>0), host_key . "#" . dkey, null())
  | eval wtrack=tonumber(toString(coalesce(wear_percent, wear_indicator, wearIndicator, "-1")))
  | eval stream="hist"
] 
| fillnull value=0 f1 f24 f7
| stats sum(f1) AS failures_1h sum(f24) AS failures_24h sum(f7) AS failures_7d dc(eval(if(stream="inv", dkey, null()))) AS current_disk_count max(wtrack) AS max_wear_raw values(eval(if(isnotnull(m_pred), m_pred, null()))) AS m_pred_list BY cluster_name, vendor
| eval predictive_failure_disks=if(isnotnull(m_pred_list), mvcount(m_pred_list), 0)
| eval worst_wear_pct=if((isnotnull(max_wear_raw)) AND (max_wear_raw>=0) AND (max_wear_raw<=100), max_wear_raw, null())
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup disk_owner | eval cluster_name=toString(cluster_name) | fields cluster_name, expected_disk_count, change_freeze_until, business_service, owner_email, redundancy_failures_allowed, default_rf ]
| eval expected_disk_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(expected_disk_count, -1)))
| eval rfa=tonumber(toString(coalesce(redundancy_failures_allowed, "1")))
| eval disk_loss=if((isnotnull(expected_disk_count)) AND (expected_disk_count>0) AND (isnotnull(current_disk_count)) AND (expected_disk_count>current_disk_count), expected_disk_count - current_disk_count, 0)
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft)) AND (now()<cft), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    (failures_1h>1) OR (disk_loss>rfa) OR (failures_7d>3) OR (predictive_failure_disks>2) OR (isnotnull(worst_wear_pct) AND (worst_wear_pct>=100)),
    "critical",
    (failures_1h=1) OR (predictive_failure_disks>=2),
    "high",
    (predictive_failure_disks=1) OR (isnotnull(worst_wear_pct) AND (worst_wear_pct>=90)) OR (disk_loss>0),
    "medium",
    (failures_24h>0) OR (failures_7d>0),
    "low",
    1=1,
    "ok"
  )
| eval sev0=if((isnull(sev0)) OR (sev0=""), "ok", sev0)
| eval severity_bucket=if(
    (freeze_active=0),
    sev0,
    if(
      (sev0="critical"),
      "critical",
      if(
        (sev0="high"),
        "medium",
        if((sev0="medium"), "low", if((sev0="low"), "ok", if((sev0="ok"), "ok", sev0)))
      )
    )
  )
| eval sord=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="high",2, severity_bucket="medium",3, severity_bucket="low",4, severity_bucket="ok",5,1=1,6)
| table cluster_name, vendor, current_disk_count, expected_disk_count, disk_loss, failures_1h, failures_24h, failures_7d, predictive_failure_disks, worst_wear_pct, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, sord
| sort 0 sord, cluster_name
| fields - sord
```

Document in the runbook: any edit to the `is_fail` case list must re-test against historical VMware upgrade notes, because vCenter may add new vSAN `eventType` strings without marketing fanfare. Keep AND/OR in uppercase inside `eval` if you add further Boolean clauses on separate lines.

Step 3 — Validate

1. VMware vSphere — open the vSAN cluster, go to Monitor, vSAN, then Physical Disks, and read each drive’s `health` band against the latest `vmware:vsan:diskgroup` event with `api_path=disks` in the same five-minute window as your poller. If Splunk is green but a disk is `UnhealthyOffline` in the UI, you filtered out `object_kind` in the first multisearch subquery or the session token expired; fix the poller before you tune severity.
2. From a jump host, run `curl --cacert /path/chain.pem -H "vmware-api-session-id: <sid>" "https://<vcenter-fqdn>/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/<cluster-mo-or-guid>/disks"` and compare the JSON count to `current_disk_count` after your normalisation. A shorter JSON array with no matching change ticket is the `disk_loss` case this UC is for.
3. Nutanix Prism or Prism Central — navigate to Hardware, Disk, and compare `disk_state` to `nutanix:cluster:disks`. Also open Alerts for `DiskFailure` or `DiskOffline` in the same wall-clock hour as a raised `failures_1h` in Splunk; titles must align or you are double-counting older alerts.
4. HyperFlex Connect — go to System Information, Disks, and compare the `healthState` and serial field to the `cisco:hyperflex:disk` line. `curl --cacert ... -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" "https://<hx>/rest/clusters/<id>/disks"` with the same read-only user as production must return bytes that parse to the same fields. If the REST body disagrees with Splunk, suspect HEC or TLS interception on the forward path, not the array.
5. If `disk_owner.expected_disk_count` is higher than the UI, your CMDB still lists RMAs that already finished; if lower, a shelf expansion is not in CMDB. Disagreement between Splunk and a vendor UI is never ignored: either the data contract drifted, or a human has not yet updated inventory.

This checklist is different from the UC-19.2.25 Prism AHV proof and the UC-19.2.1 HyperFlex resiliency proof because the unit under test is the physical disk, not a service map or a cluster-wide `health` sentence.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Row A — a single value for `sum(failures_24h)` in a timechart and another for all clusters’ `predictive_failure_disks` over the lookback, bound to a red threshold when non-zero. Row B — the Step 2 `table` on a short refresh for the NOC, with a drill to raw. Row C — a timechart of failure rates per `vendor` to show if one family spiked. Row D — a Sankey or `xyseries` matrix from a summary of `sourcetype` × `is_fail` to catch double-counts early. Row E — optional `nix:smart` wear when index=os is licensed for that scope.

Route `severity_bucket=critical` and any `disk_loss>0` to PagerDuty with the cluster and service owner, route `high` to ServiceNow as P2 with the same owner tag, `medium` to Slack, and `low` to a daily email digest. For ITSI, build one service per `cluster_name`, bind KPIs for `current_disk_count` versus `expected_disk_count`, a KPI for `predictive_failure_disks`, and a third for `worst_wear_pct` with adaptive thresholds, then link child entities to UCS or the Nutanix block serial when you have that integration.

Runbook: a single `diskFailed` in the small hours is replace-and-verify with hardware dispatch; `two` unhealthy disks on the same host` is a higher fan-out risk—check fault domains before you close the ticket; `cluster-wide wear` near vendor limits (watch `worst_wear_pct` and SMART if enabled) is a purchasing conversation, not a one-disk hot swap, and you should raise capacity and vendor engagement before the next finance freeze.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

a. Empty `table` after upgrade: a macro alias for `comment` is missing, or the multisearch arm returned zero; run each bracket as its own `search` with `| head 20` to see which `sourcetype` is empty.

c. `current_disk_count` is zero for a live cluster: the inventory arm never matched—check that `object_kind=physical_disk` is present, or that your Nutanix input still runs after a PC move.

d. `disk_loss` always equals zero while you see missing disks: `expected_disk_count` in the lookup is `-1` or null; set real integers.

e. All rows stuck at `critical` because `failures_7d` is huge: you are counting routine `vsan.host.diskAdded` in `is_fail`—tighten the `et` list so adds are not failures.

f. No predictive column even when Prism shows kPredictiveFailure: the multisearch coalesce for `dstate` is wrong; check `| fieldsummary` on the raw stream.

`g.` `worst_wear_pct` is null on all-flash vSAN: `wear_percent` is not in the HEC line; re-open the vSphere 8 `/disks` sample and add the correct key path, or add SMART via `nix:smart` for Linux hosts that sit on the same tray.

`h.` Freeze is active but you still get high PagerDuty volume: the demotion only applies to non-critical; check that the freeze string parses—bad timezone offsets in ISO text break `cft`.

`i.` Double rows from the same RMA: both `vmware:vc:event` and `vmware:vsan:health` see the same fault; de-duplicate with `| dedup` on a composite of `cluster_name` and `dkey` in a summary index, or keep only one of the families for the primary alert.

`j.` vSAN API timing: if polls align with a heavy vCenter backup, 429s may drop whole clusters; your Python sample exits early on 429; back off and alert on `ExecProcessor` gaps.

`k.` Nutanix list pagination: if PC returns paged `next_cursor` JSON, your TA must follow pages or `current_disk_count` under-reports; compare `dc(dkey)` to Prism’s count.

`l.` HyperFlex `401` on token: refresh `POST /rest/aaa/login` more often; log token age next to 401 in `splunkd`.

`m.` SMART never contributes: `nix:smart` is not in `index=os` for that host class; expect null wear and lean on the array APIs only.

`n.` Extending to NetApp HCI or PowerStore: emit your own `netapp:hci:disk` or `dell:powerstore:drive` with the same `cluster_name` and `dkey` rules, add a new multisearch sub-arm with `earliest=-2h@h`, and extend the `vendor` `case`—do not rename existing sourcetypes mid-flight. Document the new families beside this UC, not as silent edits. When two teams both push `vmware:vc:event`, align indexes so you are not re-licensing the same line twice. After each vendor major release, re-run the Step 3 curl set and re-sign the runbook in your change system so auditors see the dated proof.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.5 — HCI physical disk failure: multisearch with earliest=-2h@h for inventory, earliest=-7d@d for failure; join uses inputlookup disk_owner; five-tier case severity; freeze does not down-class critical.")`
| multisearch [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval ap=toString(coalesce(api_path, apiPath, object_kind, objectKind, ""))
  | where (ap="disks") OR (ap="physical_disk") OR match(ap, "(?i)physical") OR ((isnull(diskGroupUuid) OR len(toString(diskGroupUuid))=0) AND (isnotnull(naa) OR isnotnull(deviceId) OR isnotnull(device_id)))
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, clusterId, vcenter_cluster, ""))
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_key, device_key, naa, name, id, deviceId, device_id, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="vmware"
  | eval stream="inv"
  | eval wtrack=tonumber(toString(coalesce(wear_percent, wear_indicator, wearIndicator, "-1")))
] [ search (index=nutanix OR index=hci) sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, "unknown"))
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_uuid, diskUuid, disk_serial, diskSerial, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="nutanix"
  | eval stream="inv"
  | eval wtrack=-1
] [ search (index=hyperflex OR index=hci) sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:disk" earliest=-2h@h
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name, displayName, cluster_id, "unknown"))
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_uuid, serial, diskSerial, id, name, "unknown"))
  | eval vendor="cisco"
  | eval stream="inv"
  | eval wtrack=-1
] [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci OR index=nutanix OR index=hyperflex OR index=os) earliest=-7d@d
    (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:disk" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm" OR (index=os AND sourcetype="nix:smart"))
  | eval et=toString(coalesce(event_type, EventType, type, name, eventType, alert_title, title, message, test_id, ""))
  | eval cluster_name=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, clusterId, vcenter_cluster, "unknown"))
  | eval dstate=toString(coalesce(disk_state, diskState, ""))
  | eval hraw=lower(toString(coalesce('health.state', health_state, healthState, "")))
  | eval hxi=tonumber(toString(coalesce(healthState, health_state, "0")))
  | eval mfr=tonumber(toString(coalesce(marked_for_removal, markedForRemoval, "0")))
  | eval vendor=case(sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR match(sourcetype, "^vmware:"), "vmware", sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks", "nutanix", match(sourcetype, "cisco:hyperflex"), "cisco", sourcetype="nix:smart", "os_smart", 1=1, "other")
  | eval dkey=toString(coalesce(disk_key, device_key, disk_uuid, diskUuid, naa, name, id, deviceId, device_id, "unknown"))
  | eval is_fail=case(sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" AND (et IN ("LostStorageEvent", "DegradedHostStorageEvent") OR match(et, "vsan\.host\.diskFailed")), 1, sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" AND match(lower(et), "diskfailure|diskoffline|diskmarked|disk.*fail"), 1, sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" AND dstate IN ("kOffline"), 1, (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:diskgroup" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:disks" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:disk") AND ((match(hraw, "unhealthy") AND (match(hraw, "online") OR match(hraw, "offline"))) OR dstate IN ("kPredictiveFailure", "kMarkedForRemoval") OR mfr=1 OR hxi>2), 1, sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:alarm" AND match(lower(et), "disk|media|storage|drive|ssd|nvme"), 1, 1=1, 0)
  | eval is_pred=case(dstate="kPredictiveFailure", 1, mfr=1 AND dstate!="kNormal", 1, hxi=2 OR hxi=3, 1, match(hraw, "unhealthy|predictive"), 1, 1=1, 0)
  | eval f1=if((is_fail=1) AND _time>=relative_time(now(), "-1h@h"), 1, 0)
  | eval f24=if((is_fail=1) AND _time>=relative_time(now(), "-24h@h"), 1, 0)
  | eval f7=if(is_fail=1, 1, 0)
  | eval host_key=toString(coalesce(host, hostName, node_name, esx_host, "unknown"))
  | eval m_pred=if((is_pred=1) AND (len(host_key)>0), host_key . "#" . dkey, null())
  | eval wtrack=tonumber(toString(coalesce(wear_percent, wear_indicator, wearIndicator, "-1")))
  | eval stream="hist"
] 
| fillnull value=0 f1 f24 f7
| stats sum(f1) AS failures_1h sum(f24) AS failures_24h sum(f7) AS failures_7d dc(eval(if(stream="inv", dkey, null()))) AS current_disk_count max(wtrack) AS max_wear_raw values(eval(if(isnotnull(m_pred), m_pred, null()))) AS m_pred_list BY cluster_name, vendor
| eval predictive_failure_disks=if(isnotnull(m_pred_list), mvcount(m_pred_list), 0)
| eval worst_wear_pct=if((isnotnull(max_wear_raw)) AND (max_wear_raw>=0) AND (max_wear_raw<=100), max_wear_raw, null())
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name [ | inputlookup disk_owner | eval cluster_name=toString(cluster_name) | fields cluster_name, expected_disk_count, change_freeze_until, business_service, owner_email, redundancy_failures_allowed, default_rf ]
| eval expected_disk_count=tonumber(toString(coalesce(expected_disk_count, -1)))
| eval rfa=tonumber(toString(coalesce(redundancy_failures_allowed, "1")))
| eval disk_loss=if((isnotnull(expected_disk_count)) AND (expected_disk_count>0) AND (isnotnull(current_disk_count)) AND (expected_disk_count>current_disk_count), expected_disk_count - current_disk_count, 0)
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(cft)) AND (now()<cft), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    (failures_1h>1) OR (disk_loss>rfa) OR (failures_7d>3) OR (predictive_failure_disks>2) OR (isnotnull(worst_wear_pct) AND (worst_wear_pct>=100)),
    "critical",
    (failures_1h=1) OR (predictive_failure_disks>=2),
    "high",
    (predictive_failure_disks=1) OR (isnotnull(worst_wear_pct) AND (worst_wear_pct>=90)) OR (disk_loss>0),
    "medium",
    (failures_24h>0) OR (failures_7d>0),
    "low",
    1=1,
    "ok"
  )
| eval sev0=if((isnull(sev0)) OR (sev0=""), "ok", sev0)
| eval severity_bucket=if(
    (freeze_active=0),
    sev0,
    if(
      (sev0="critical"),
      "critical",
      if(
        (sev0="high"),
        "medium",
        if((sev0="medium"), "low", if((sev0="low"), "ok", if((sev0="ok"), "ok", sev0)))
      )
    )
  )
| eval sord=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="high",2, severity_bucket="medium",3, severity_bucket="low",4, severity_bucket="ok",5,1=1,6)
| table cluster_name, vendor, current_disk_count, expected_disk_count, disk_loss, failures_1h, failures_24h, failures_7d, predictive_failure_disks, worst_wear_pct, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, sord
| sort 0 sord, cluster_name
| fields - sord
```

## Visualization

Row 1: two single value panels, total `failures_24h` across clusters and `predictive_failure_disks` across clusters. Row 2: a sortable `table` of the Step 2 output fields with colour on `severity_bucket` and a drill to raw per vendor. Row 3: a `timechart` of `sum(f7)`-equivalent failure rate (from a child search) per day with overlays for maintenance. Row 4: a Sankey or `xyseries` matrix of `vendor` × `severity_bucket` or failure source family, fed from a summary or hourly scheduled search, not a live seven-day raw rescan. Row 5: optional `nix:smart` overlay panel when `index=os` SMART exists.

- [Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)
- [VMware vSphere — vCenter REST and automation documentation](https://developer.broadcom.com/xapis/vsphere-automation-api/latest/)
- [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833) for nix:smart](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)
